title: Stryker Rewatches Evangelion SRE
wikitext:
[]: Stryker Rewatches Evangelion (SRE)             Quote Post by Stryker » Sat Dec 01, 2012 7:22 pm Turn's out there is no general thread where people post their feels about what they watched or specific occurences when they rewatch it. Sure, there is the Commentary , but I'm not sure Reichu or the other admins want to reopen that. . . So to business. I'm planning to rewatch Evangelion. And you're going to hear about it. In this thread. Oh, and I post my feels about what happens, so there's that too. You can join me if you want, and post your feels too. I'ma do it tonight.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Dec 01, 2012 7:32 pm It's still too soon for me to go back and re-watch NGE. Marathoning it in one night was one of the worse ideas I've ever had in a long history of bad ideas. Can't wait to see if marathoning the new movies will be just as bad as an idea for similar reasons.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sat Dec 01, 2012 10:55 pm Notes. BTW I took these notes at the time I was within the show, not after finishing the show. Watching this with Japanese dub w/ Renewal subs. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sat Dec 01, 2012 11:11 pm Wait, is this your first time watching the series subbed rather than dubbed, Stryker? If that's the case this thread just got a whole lot more interesting....

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sat Dec 01, 2012 11:37 pm This should be interesting... Stryker wrote: (13:00): That hand in the background is bound to make a comeback, isn't it?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Dec 02, 2012 2:32 am Stryker wrote:

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Sun Dec 02, 2012 2:34 am Stryker wrote: (2:04): There is a child running across some railroad tracks. What's up with this?

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sun Dec 02, 2012 3:27 am Blue Monday wrote: I don't understand why anybody watches anything in a "marathon" session. It's just plain unenjoyable, at least for me. I'd get far too reckless and it would only detract from the overall viewing experience. A couple episodes (three or four at most) in a row is more than enough, isn't it?

[]:             Quote Post by Chrad » Sun Dec 02, 2012 9:16 am CJD wrote: Wait what? I don't remember that.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Dec 02, 2012 9:44 am Mr. Tines wrote: Aspect ratio! The series is 4:3 TV from the 90s, not 16:9 widescreen; so those screen grabs are a little painful to look at.

[]:             Quote Post by jimmiel » Sun Dec 02, 2012 10:50 am Blue Monday wrote: Hah! Reminds me of that episode of The Simpsons where the comet/asteroid is coming, and some dude just runs across the street and hides under a bench, haha. I don't understand why anybody watches anything in a "marathon" session. It's just plain unenjoyable, at least for me. I'd get far too reckless and it would only detract from the overall viewing experience. A couple episodes (three or four at most) in a row is more than enough, isn't it?

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Dec 02, 2012 6:16 pm It is too deep. It's a lot to absorb in little time.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sun Dec 02, 2012 6:27 pm Stryker wrote: It is too deep. It's a lot to absorb in little time.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sun Dec 02, 2012 7:14 pm Just want to say something regarding marathoning anime.... riffraff11235 wrote: Marathoning an anime for your first viewing probably isn't a good idea.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sun Dec 02, 2012 9:00 pm @CJD Looking back at it now, I'm not sure of what I said anymore, since I tend to do exactly what you described when I get into an anime. Time permitting, of course. It really is a subjective thing, though. Some people like to take some time to digest what they've read so far, while others like to march on and read/watch as much as possible. I do a bit of both.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Dec 02, 2012 9:49 pm Fixed up aspect ratio so it isn't as painful. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Mon Dec 03, 2012 12:06 am Stryker wrote: (1:40): Am I the only amused that in the Japanese dub, Kotono Mitsuishi feels it necessary to say, "LIFT OFF" in English?

[]:             Quote Post by MAGI_01 » Mon Dec 03, 2012 2:08 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Marathoning it in one night was one of the worse ideas I've ever had in a long history of bad ideas. Can't wait to see if marathoning the new movies will be just as bad as an idea for similar reasons.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Mon Dec 03, 2012 7:51 am Stryker wrote: (7:27): Not exactly hand imagery, but you can tell that Shinji is contemplating about what happened (to him), or perhaps even what he did.

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Mon Dec 03, 2012 8:37 am Stryker wrote: (21:26) Don't worry, I was just as disturbed as you were, Shinji. Just not as screamy. I'm in a house of sleeping people; can't afford to be screamy.
